content.text:
# Development Environment
SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=dev
SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://db:5432/spring_template_dev
POSTGRES_DB=spring_template_dev
POSTGRES_USER=development
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=T3st!ng

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
    <parent>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
        <version>4.0.0</version>
        <relativePath/>
    </parent>

    <groupId>com.tzesh</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-template</artifactId>
    <version>2.2-SNAPSHOT</version>
    <name>spring-boot-template</name>
    <description>spring-boot-template</description>

    <properties>
        <java.version>25</java.version>
        <org.mapstruct.version>1.6.3</org.mapstruct.version>
        <org.projectlombok.version>1.18.42</org.projectlombok.version>
    </properties>

    <dependencies>
        <!-- JPA Data -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Spring HATEOAS -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-hateoas</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- JSON Web Token -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.jsonwebtoken</groupId>
            <artifactId>jjwt-api</artifactId>
            <version>0.13.0</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.jsonwebtoken</groupId>
            <artifactId>jjwt-impl</artifactId>
            <version>0.13.0</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.jsonwebtoken</groupId>
            <artifactId>jjwt-jackson</artifactId>
            <version>0.13.0</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Spring Security -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-security</artifactId>
            <exclusions>
                <exclusion>
                    <artifactId>logback-classic</artifactId>
                    <groupId>ch.qos.logback</groupId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <artifactId>log4j-over-slf4j</artifactId>
                    <groupId>org.slf4j</groupId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-logging</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Spring Web Validation -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Spring Web -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
            <exclusions>
                <exclusion>
                    <artifactId>logback-classic</artifactId>
                    <groupId>ch.qos.logback</groupId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <artifactId>log4j-over-slf4j</artifactId>
                    <groupId>org.slf4j</groupId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-logging</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Springdoc OpenAPI -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springdoc</groupId>
            <artifactId>springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui</artifactId>
            <version>3.0.2</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- DevTools -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-devtools</artifactId>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
            <optional>true</optional>
        </dependency>

        <!-- postgresql -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.postgresql</groupId>
            <artifactId>postgresql</artifactId>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Configuration Processor -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-configuration-processor</artifactId>
            <optional>true</optional>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Project lombok -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
            <version>${org.projectlombok.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Test -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Byte Buddy (required by Hibernate 7.x for bytecode enhancement) -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>net.bytebuddy</groupId>
            <artifactId>byte-buddy</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Mapstruct -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.mapstruct</groupId>
            <artifactId>mapstruct</artifactId>
            <version>${org.mapstruct.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Log4j2 -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-log4j2</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Log4j2 JSON Template Layout (for structured logging) -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.logging.log4j</groupId>
            <artifactId>log4j-layout-template-json</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- H2 Database -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.h2database</groupId>
            <artifactId>h2</artifactId>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <dependencyManagement>
        <dependencies>
            <dependency>
                <groupId>com.tzesh</groupId>
                <artifactId>SpringBootCore</artifactId>
                <version>1.0</version>
            </dependency>
        </dependencies>
    </dependencyManagement>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <!-- Maven Resources -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <excludes>
                        <exclude>
                            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
                            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
                        </exclude>
                    </excludes>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Maven Compiler -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>3.14.0</version>
                <configuration>
                    <release>25</release>
                    <compilerId>javac</compilerId>
                    <annotationProcessorPaths>
                        <!-- Please note that order of project lombok and mapstruct is important -->

                        <!-- Project lombok -->
                        <path>
                            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
                            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
                            <version>${org.projectlombok.version}</version>
                        </path>



                        <!-- Mapstruct -->
                        <path>
                            <groupId>org.mapstruct</groupId>
                            <artifactId>mapstruct-processor</artifactId>
                            <version>${org.mapstruct.version}</version>
                        </path>
                    </annotationProcessorPaths>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Maven Enforcer -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>enforce-versions</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>enforce</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <rules>
                                <requireJavaVersion>
                                    <version>[25,)</version>
                                </requireJavaVersion>
                                <requireMavenVersion>
                                    <version>[3.8.7,)</version>
                                </requireMavenVersion>
                            </rules>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- JaCoCo Code Coverage -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.jacoco</groupId>
                <artifactId>jacoco-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>0.8.14</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>prepare-agent</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>prepare-agent</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                    <execution>
                        <id>report</id>
                        <phase>test</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>report</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <modules>
        <module>spring-boot-template-core</module>
        <module>spring-boot-template-api</module>
    </modules>

    <packaging>pom</packaging>

</project>

## Base Configuration File for Spring Boot Template API
# ==========================
# This file contains the base configuration for the Spring Boot Template API.
# It includes settings for server, application, database, JPA, and JWT.
# ==========================
# Note: This file is intended to be used as a template. Modify the values as needed for your environment.
# ==========================


# Spring Boot Configuration
server.port=8080
server.servlet.context-path=/api/v1/

# Application Configuration
application.title=Spring Template
application.version=2.0

# JPA Configuration
spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update

# JWT Configuration
security.jwt.secret-key=34743777217A25432A462D4A614E645267556B58703273357638782F413F4428472B4B6250655368566D597133743677397A244226452948404D635166546A57
security.jwt.authorization-key=614E645267556B58703273357638792F423F4528482B4B6250655368566D597133743677397A24432646294A404E635166546A576E5A7234753778214125442A
security.jwt.expiration=86400000
security.jwt.refresh-expiration=604800000
security.jwt.issuer=SpringTemplate

# Rate Limit Configuration
ratelimit.enabled=true
ratelimit.limit=100
ratelimit.duration-minutes=1

# Rate Limit Category Configuration
ratelimit.strict.limit=10
ratelimit.strict.duration-minutes=1
ratelimit.standard.limit=60
ratelimit.standard.duration-minutes=1
ratelimit.relaxed.limit=200
ratelimit.relaxed.duration-minutes=1
ratelimit.authentication.limit=5
ratelimit.authentication.duration-minutes=1

# Redis Configuration
spring.data.redis.host=redis
spring.data.redis.port=6379

# Spring Session with Redis
spring.session.store-type=redis
spring.session.redis.namespace=spring:session
spring.session.timeout=30m

# Kafka Configuration
spring.kafka.bootstrap-servers=kafka:9092
spring.kafka.consumer.group-id=spring-template-group
spring.kafka.consumer.auto-offset-reset=earliest
spring.kafka.consumer.key-deserializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer
spring.kafka.consumer.value-deserializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer
spring.kafka.producer.key-serializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer
spring.kafka.producer.value-serializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer

# Kafka Logging Configuration
logging.level.org.apache.kafka=INFO
logging.level.org.springframework.kafka=INFO
# To enable debug logging for troubleshooting, use DEBUG instead of INFO
# logging.level.org.apache.kafka=DEBUG
# logging.level.org.springframework.kafka=DEBUG

# Actuator Configuration
management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,metrics
management.endpoint.health.show-details=when-authorized
management.endpoint.health.probes.enabled=true

data.content:
Let me now produce a comprehensive answer.Now I have a thorough understanding of the security architecture. Here is my answer.

---

## Security & Protected API Usage in This Template

This is a **Spring Boot 4.0** application (Java 25) that implements a full stateless security layer using **JWT authentication**, **role-based access control (RBAC)** with granular **permissions**, and additional API protection mechanisms such as **rate limiting** and **idempotency**. The security model is split across two Maven modules: `spring-boot-template-api` (REST endpoints, security config, JWT logic) and `spring-boot-template-core` (base classes, error handling, annotations).

---

### 1. JWT Authentication

**Library:** `io.jsonwebtoken:jjwt-api:0.13.0` (with `jjwt-impl` and `jjwt-jackson`).

**Service:** [`JwtService`](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/service/auth/JwtService.java)

- **Token generation:** Creates both **access tokens** (default 24h) and **refresh tokens** (default 7d), configurable via `application.properties`:
  ```properties
  security.jwt.secret-key=347437...
  security.jwt.expiration=86400000
  security.jwt.refresh-expiration=604800000
  security.jwt.issuer=SpringTemplate
  ```
- **Signing:** HMAC-SHA using a Base64-decoded secret key.
- **Claims:** The username is stored as the **subject** (`sub`); additional claims can be passed.
- **Validation:** Each token is checked for expiry, correct username match, and database persistence (see *Token Management* below).

**Filter:** [`AuthenticationFilter`](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/config/security/jwt/AuthenticationFilter.java)

- Extends `OncePerRequestFilter` and runs before `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter`.
- Skips the filter for paths containing `/api/v1/auth`.
- Extracts the JWT from the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
- Validates the token via `JwtService.isTokenValid()` **and** checks the database that the token is neither expired nor revoked (`TokenRepository`).
- On success, creates a `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken` and sets it on the `SecurityContextHolder`.

**Token Persistence:** [`TokenEntity`](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/entity/auth/TokenEntity.java) stores every JWT in the `TOKEN` table with `revoked` and `expired` boolean flags. This enables server-side logout and token revocation.

---

### 2. Security Configuration (Filter Chain)

[`SecurityChain`](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/config/security/SecurityChain.java)

```java
@Configuration
@EnableWebSecurity
@EnableMethodSecurity
public class SecurityChain { ... }
```

**Endpoint authorization rules:**
| Path pattern | Access |
|---|---|
| `/auth/**`, `/v3/api-docs/**`, `/swagger-ui/**`, `/webjars/**`, `/swagger-ui.html`, `/actuator/**` | **Permit all** (no auth required) |
| `/management/**` (any HTTP method) | `ADMIN` or `MANAGER` **role** required |
| `GET /management/**` | `ADMIN_READ` or `MANAGER_READ` **authority** |
| `POST /management/**` | `ADMIN_CREATE` or `MANAGER_CREATE` authority |
| `PUT /management/**` | `ADMIN_UPDATE` or `MANAGER_UPDATE` authority |
| `PATCH /management/**` | `ADMIN_UPDATE` or `MANAGER_UPDATE` authority |
| `DELETE /management/**` | `ADMIN_DELETE` or `MANAGER_DELETE` authority |
| Any other request | **Authenticated** (any valid token) |

- **CSRF disabled** – the API is stateless.
- **Session management** set to `STATELESS`.
- **Custom `AuthenticationEntryPoint`** returns a 401 JSON error for unauthenticated requests.
- **Custom `LogoutHandler`** (`LogoutService`) revokes the token in the database when `/auth/logout` is called.

---

### 3. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) & Permissions

**Roles:** [`Role` enum](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/enumeration/auth/Role.java)

| Role | Permissions |
|---|---|
| `USER` | *(none)* |
| `MANAGER` | `MANAGER_READ`, `MANAGER_UPDATE`, `MANAGER_DELETE`, `MANAGER_CREATE` |
| `ADMIN` | All `ADMIN_*` permissions **plus** all `MANAGER_*` permissions |

**Permissions:** [`Permission` enum](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/enumeration/auth/Permission.java)

```java
ADMIN_READ("admin:read"),    ADMIN_UPDATE("admin:update"),
ADMIN_CREATE("admin:create"), ADMIN_DELETE("admin:delete"),
MANAGER_READ("management:read"),   MANAGER_UPDATE("management:update"),
MANAGER_CREATE("management:create"), MANAGER_DELETE("management:delete");
```

Each role's `getAuthorities()` returns a list of `SimpleGrantedAuthority` objects: one `ROLE_<NAME>` for the role itself, plus one `SimpleGrantedAuthority` for each of its permissions. This allows both role-level checks (`hasRole(...)`) and permission-level checks (`hasAuthority(...)`) in `@PreAuthorize` and the filter chain.

**Method-level security (`@PreAuthorize`):**

The [`UserController`](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/controller/user/UserController.java) uses `@PreAuthorize` to restrict endpoints:

```java
@PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")       // User CRUD endpoints
@PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN') || hasRole('USER') || hasRole('MODERATOR')")  // /auth/authorize
```

---

### 4. Authentication Endpoints

[`AuthenticationController`](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/controller/auth/AuthenticationController.java) lives under `/auth`:

| Endpoint | Method | Auth Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/auth/register` | POST | No | Register a new user (defaults to `USER` role). Returns `access_token` + `refresh_token`. |
| `/auth/login` | POST | No | Authenticate with username/password. Returns tokens. |
| `/auth/refresh-token` | POST | Yes (Bearer refresh token) | Obtain a new access token using a refresh token. |
| `/auth/authorize` | POST | Yes (any role) | **Change a user’s role** (requires a server-side `authorization-key` secret – admin-only by intent). |
| `/auth/logout` | POST | Yes (Bearer token) | Revokes the current token. |

**Authentication flow:**
1. **Register** → user saved with `ROLE_USER`, BCrypt-hashed password, JWT + refresh token issued.
2. **Login** → `AuthenticationManager` verifies credentials, old tokens revoked, new tokens issued.
3. **Subsequent requests** → client sends `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` header.
4. **Token refresh** → send the refresh token as Bearer token to `/auth/refresh-token`.
5. **Logout** → the `LogoutService` marks the token as expired and revoked in the database, then clears the security context.

---

### 5. The User Entity & Spring Security Integration

[`User`](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/entity/User.java) implements `UserDetails`:

```java
@Override
public Collection<? extends GrantedAuthority> getAuthorities() {
    return role.getAuthorities();  // delegates to Role enum
}
```

The [`SecurityServices`](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/config/security/SecurityServices.java) configuration provides:

- **`UserDetailsService`**: loads a `User` by username from the database (used by Spring Security internally).
- **`AuthenticationProvider`**: a `DaoAuthenticationProvider` with BCrypt password encoder.
- **`PasswordEncoder`**: `BCryptPasswordEncoder`.
- **`AuthenticationManager`**: from the `AuthenticationConfiguration`.

---

### 6. Error Handling for Security

| Scenario | Handler | HTTP Status | Response Body |
|---|---|---|---|
| No token / invalid token | [`AuthenticationEntryPoint`](spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/config/security/jwt/AuthenticationEntryPoint.java) | **401 Unauthorized** | `BaseResponse` with `GenericErrorMessage` |
| Valid token but insufficient role | [`AccessDeniedExceptionHandler`](spring-boot-template-core/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/base/handler/AccessDeniedExceptionHandler.java) | **403 Forbidden** | `BaseResponse` with `GenericErrorMessage` |
| Expired / revoked token | `AuthenticationFilter` (no context set) | 401 (via entry point) | JSON error |

The custom [`UnauthorizedException`](spring-boot-template-core/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/base/exception/UnauthorizedException.java) (annotated `@ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)`) can be thrown programmatically.

---

### 7. Additional API Protection Mechanisms

**Rate Limiting:**
Custom annotations [`@RateLimit`](spring-boot-template-core/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/base/annotation/RateLimit.java) and [`@RateLimitCategory`](spring-boot-template-core/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/base/annotation/RateLimitCategory.java) allow tiered rate limiting per endpoint or controller. Categories include `AUTHENTICATION` (5/min), `STRICT` (10/min), `STANDARD` (60/min), and `RELAXED` (200/min), configured in `application.properties`.

**Idempotency:**
The [`@Idempotent`](spring-boot-template-core/src/main/java/com/tzesh/springtemplate/base/annotation/Idempotent.java) annotation (with configurable TTL) prevents duplicate processing of the same request (e.g., accidental double-submits on registration).

**Input Validation:**
All request DTOs use Jakarta Validation annotations (`@NotEmpty`, `@Email`, `@Length`, etc.) validated via `@Valid` on controller parameters.

---

### 8. How Users Are Expected to Interact with Secured Endpoints

1. **Obtain tokens:**
   - Call `POST /auth/register` (new users) or `POST /auth/login` (existing users).
   - Receive `{ "access_token": "...", "refresh_token": "..." }`.

2. **Call protected APIs:**
   - Include the access token in the `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` header.
   - Accessible endpoints depend on the user’s **role**:
     - `USER` → can access `/auth/**`, `/users/current`, and any other authenticated endpoint not restricted to `ADMIN`/`MANAGER`.
     - `MANAGER` → additionally can access `/management/**` with granular read/write/update/delete permissions.
     - `ADMIN` → full access, including user management (`/users/**`) and role authorization.

3. **Refresh tokens:**
   - When the access token expires, call `POST /auth/refresh-token` with the refresh token as the Bearer token.
   - A new access token is returned (the old one is revoked).

4. **Logout:**
   - Call `POST /auth/logout` with the current token; the token is revoked server-side and cannot be reused.

5. **Error handling:**
   - **401** → token missing, expired, revoked, or invalid → re-authenticate.
   - **403** → token valid but insufficient privileges for the endpoint.
   - **429** → rate limit exceeded; retry after the configured window.

---

**Summary:** This template provides a production-ready Spring Boot security foundation with JWT-based stateless authentication, role/permission-based authorization (USER/MANAGER/ADMIN), token revocation via database persistence, rate limiting, idempotency, structured error responses, and OpenAPI documentation. Developers can extend it by adding new roles/permissions, modifying the filter-chain rules in `SecurityChain`, or protecting additional endpoints with `@PreAuthorize`.
